# Per-Hypothesis Context: H-E1
# Generated by Phase 2C JIT Context Generation from 02b_verification_plan.md

## Hypothesis Information

- **ID:** H-E1
- **Type:** EXISTENCE
- **Gate:** MUST_WORK
- **Prerequisites:** None (foundation)
- **Status:** IN_PROGRESS

## Hypothesis Statement

Under pinned benchmark and corpus versions, if 13-gram containment rates are computed across all 57 MMLU sub-tasks + HellaSwag + BIG-Bench Hard against The Pile v1, then contamination rates will vary significantly across sub-tasks (Kruskal-Wallis p < 0.05) because benchmark sub-tasks span different academic and commonsense domains that are unevenly represented in training corpora.

## Success Criteria (PoC: Direction-based)

- **Primary:** Kruskal-Wallis p < 0.05 for sub-task variance across 59 benchmark sub-tasks
- **Secondary:** At least one sub-task pair shows > 5 percentage point contamination rate difference

## Experimental Setup (from Phase 2B Section 1.3)

| Component | Selection | Justification |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| **Dataset** | MMLU (cais/mmlu v1.0.0) + HellaSwag (Rowan/hellaswag) + BIG-Bench Hard (lukaemon/bbh) × The Pile v1 (EleutherAI/pile) | Primary benchmarks studied in contamination literature; enables comparison with WIMBD results |
| **Model** | N/A — allenai/wimbd + custom Python (MinHash LSH) | CPU-only n-gram analysis; no model weights needed |

**Text Format:** question+choices concatenation (WIMBD standard)
**N-gram size:** 13 (WIMBD standard)
**Total sub-tasks:** 59 (57 MMLU + HellaSwag + BIG-Bench Hard)

## Verification Protocol

1. Load all 59 benchmark sub-tasks from HF (cais/mmlu, Rowan/hellaswag, lukaemon/bbh); format as question+choices concatenation
2. Build MinHash LSH index for The Pile v1 via allenai/wimbd streaming
3. Query index for each test instance; aggregate 13-gram containment rate per sub-task
4. Run Kruskal-Wallis H-test on contamination rates across 59 sub-tasks
5. Verify at least one sub-task pair shows contamination rate difference > 5 percentage points

## Baseline Comparison Targets

| Method | Performance | Dataset |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| WIMBD 13-gram containment (Pile × MMLU) | ~1-10% for some MMLU sub-tasks | The Pile v1 × MMLU |

## Failure Response

IF fails: PIVOT — reassess whether 13-gram granularity is appropriate; try shorter n-gram (n=8); re-examine text preprocessing

## Key Risks (Relevant to H-E1)

- **R1 (Medium):** Benchmark version mismatch — Pin versions; sanity-check vs. WIMBD
- **R2 (High):** 13-gram proxy invalidity — Spot-check; frame as n-gram overlap
- **R4 (Medium):** MinHash approximation error — Validate against exact; increase hash count
- **R5 (Low):** Text format assumption — Sensitivity analysis question-only vs. +choices
